tasks.description: Source and evaluate new high-quality tickers using the stock_screener tool (at least 3 rounds, including ETFs).

subtasks.description: Round 1: Call stock_screener with constraints for low beta, high ROE, moderate PE to find strong candidates.
Round 2: Call stock_screener again, altering industry/sector focus or constraints, including ETF search.
Round 3: Call stock_screener with value or growth emphasis targeting diversification or desired exposures.
Select new tickers that best match risk/reward/low correlation needs (no tool call).

tasks.description: Construct new/optimized portfolio, log thesis, and run analytics.

subtasks.description: Define the new/optimized portfolio in strict JSON with allocations, tickers, positions, and thesis (no tool call).
Call episodic_remember to store the new/optimized portfolio specification.
Call calculate_portfolio_performance with the new portfolio_dict for risk/return metrics.
Call calculate_portfolio_correlation_matrix to reassess correlation of the optimized portfolio.

tasks.description: Iterate: Make small portfolio tweaks based on analytics and run further performance/correlation checks.

subtasks.description: If analytics indicate improvement is needed, repeat screening and adjustment for final optimization (tools as needed).
Finalize and output the optimized portfolio in strict JSON format as specified (no tool call).

tasks.description: Formatting: Ensure output adheres to strict JSON requirements and includes 'changes' section per instructions.

subtasks.description: Double-check that the output matches the required JSON structure and explicitly details all changes (added/removed/adjusted).

execution_history.reason: Strong and weak positions have been identified using risk/return metrics and latest news sentiment—FR, IIPR, STAG, TRNO are persistent weak spots with sector-level and company-specific concerns (negative Sharpe, high drawdown, low growth) despite some idiosyncratic resilience, while many tech, utility, and thematic ETF positions remain portfolio strengths.

observations: Comprehensive risk-adjusted return and volatility metrics gathered for all holdings.
Extensive news/sentiment analysis completed for weakest sector: industrial REITs—FR, IIPR, STAG, TRNO, and EQIX.

Evidence of strong fundamental recoveries and secular tailwinds in several REITs despite sector volatility, but cluster-wide headwinds persist for industrial REIT growth, dividend safety, and rental spreads. EQIX’s sentiment is notably robust among data center REITs, while IIPR faces tenant stress and possible dividend risk, and FR flags sluggish growth/negative risk-adjusted metrics.

Combination of quantitative (Sharpe, max drawdown, alpha, volatility) and qualitative (news sentiment, sector/tenant exposure) analysis clearly separates durable, momentum, and high-upside positions from persistent laggards/riskiest cluster.

execution_history.reason: Evidence from metrics, stress testing, correlation, and news/sentiment supports keeping top innovative secular growth leaders (AI/semi/software/commodity plays), cutting weak industrial REITs, trimming speculative microcaps, and rebalancing crowded bank/ETF exposure. Diversity, beta, and correlation all considered for keep/drop proposals.

observations: High-correlation cluster found in industrial REITs (FR, STAG, TRNO; r > 0.8); IIPR also clusters in stress. FR, STAG, and TRNO all have negative or near-zero Sharpe, lag industry/benchmark returns, and recent news reveals weak relative earnings, low growth, and sector headwinds—clear drop/swap candidates. IIPR remains news-volatile and draws ongoing tenant/default/dividend risk; also signals negative Sharpe and negative stress resilience.
EQIX (data center REIT) shows strong momentum, secular tailwinds (AI/data center/green bond leadership), and improving performance—keep/raise allocation justified.

APP, AVGO, ARM, NVDA, PLTR: high Sharpe, strong growth, strong scenario resilience (except deep crash), top risk-adjusted anchors—keep and consider increasing allocation as cluster correlation is manageable relative to diversified tech/thematic weighting.

DGNX, FUFU, EXOD: all highly speculative and volatile, but at sub-1.5% allocations each; DGNX especially displays stress beta extremes and catastrophic performance in stagflation since it is deeply negative correlated to much of the rest—size down significantly or drop outright.
SGDM/SLVR: show strong commodity tailwind, low cross-correlation, and strong stress performance; keep (may increase as non-equity diversifier).
IGSB/VCSH: preserve capital in credit/fixed income dislocations. Gross exposure and risk is 0.93, all long; ensure new holdings add more downside hedge with low correlation to growth/REITs.

EMQQ/EIS/GREK/NLR: thematic and emerging market ETFs, mostly resilient and low correlation. Keep preferred names with scenario resilience and positive Sharpe, trim or cut those with weak risk-adjusted metrics and high drawdown (EMQQ).

Banks (JPM, FITB, INTR, NU): performance is varied, with JPM strong risk/return, but high correlation with FITB, INTR, and large downside in credit/banking stress. Keep JPM (anchor), size down or cut other weaker alpha or correlated bank names.
